title_guess: France: Burgundy, Lyon, Le Puy, Ardèche canoeing (Cevennes)

pages.text:
& the sun came out.
    Charles has gone to play foot ball. Simon is making the foam in our seats more comfortable.
    In the evening John chopped the Salad (and such a salad!) Then we started teaching John + Charles Bridge. Good.
    Rain in the night.
Tuesday we packed in the rain. We found a beautiful spot off the motor-way by a new pond for elevenses, & arrived home at 1 pm.

__Sunday July 7th.__
    At 10.30. Simon, Eve, John, Charles, Kalgin picked up Annie Crews with grandson, Tom, + French friend, Thomas. We set off in drizzles but the hot sun shone by 1.30. We parked at Clent & walked around the hills. Kalgin went for a run. We had a nice picnic lunch - home-made elderflower cordial, & set off home to watch Wimbledon.
Monday July 29th.
    Two days after arriving back from Thorperness we have left for France. Simon, Eve, John, Charles. Peter will fly to Lyons next

weekend to join us. Thomas is looking after 86 for 3 weeks. William is in S. America (John Warburton is his emergency number.)
    It is blazingly hot. We left at 8.05. Long hold ups on the M25. Simon lost his glasses on the boat (Eve moved seats twice because of shadows flickering.) so he is wearing his prescription sunglasses.
    At 7.15. we arrived at __Vendeuil__ south of St. Quentin. Hot, sweaty. We had a refreshing cold shower when we arrived. You pulled the chain & the water came down until you stopped pulling. The boys played ping pong.

Tuesday
    We left after breakfast & arrived at 7.45. pm Chaumard [illegible]. At lunch time we were next to a supermarket with a large [?over] for shade over the carpark. The morning was very hot & sweaty but it was cooler in the afternoon. There was a road diversion & Charles misread the map once.
    There is a large lake here & a hydro electric dam. Old flooded valleys.

__Wednesday__
	[photo postcard: Paysage du Morvan]
	[caption beside photo:] Lac de Pannesière (Nièvre)
	• our campsite at Chaumont

It rained in the night & there were intermittent showers during the day. Wonderfully cool. Eve swam out into the lake before breakfast. After breakfast John & Charles paddled/rowed right across the lake.

captions: Badoit Source St-Galmier — 100 cl Eau Minérale Naturelle Gazeuse
pages.text:
After elevenses we walked up into the village, leaving John at the van. There was a parade & bugle in the Cemetery to commemorate the Maquis of Chaumard. People were drinking in the Mairie. The church is old with old Statues of Sts Peter, Dennis, Sebastian & a very old statue of St. Anne (?).   brown slugs
																	     blond cows
	After lunch we walked up to see St. Peter's well along lovely old roads. Good views of the lake. Three hamlets including 70 buildings were drowned in building the dam 1936-49. The dam is hydro electric, built on granite, to prevent flooding of the Seine near Paris. High in July low in October. 90% water leaves the dam every year.
	[Badoit water label glued in]
	We booked croissants for breakfast & a

table for supper. The food was delicious
1lse flottant.
    Simon + Eve would give this campsite
5star, lake + lots of walks, but John + Charles
insist on only 4 star - the view's blocked by
trees, you have to pay for showers + the croissants

were stale. They are sold, after ordering,
from a depot by a woman who also runs
the fishing tackle shop.
    __Thursday__
        More drizzle during the night + morn.
John walked up to get the bread. Charles
boated. Eve swam. After breakfast at 10.20.
we left.
    Charles + Simon shopped at a supermarket.
At 1.50. there was a metalic banging so
we stopped + had lunch. Simon discovered
that one of the straps holding on the water
tank was loose. A nut worked loose +
weakened a thread in the body of the van,
possibly. He tied it on with string.
    We approached Clung, + Eve was
pleased to go. John said "Oh yes, Clung:
I've seen him in 2 films." and that about
summed up John's + Charles' interest in the
Abbey. It was poorly sign-posted once we
had paid for the tickets + the adolescent
boys were a pain to drag round. It was
difficult to figure out where to go + we

captions: Cluny Abbey in the Cornstore. From the cloister of Cluny III. The "bee-keeper", from the representation of the Winds.
pages.text: Cluny Abbey in the Cornstore. From the cloister of Cluny III. The "bee-keeper", from the representation of the Winds.

ended up missing the museum.. the Cornstore ground floor was beautiful. In its heyday 3,000 abbeys kowtowed to Cluny. But it was hammered in 1798. The town is pretty, some 18 buildings. touristy.

	After ice cream in the van we set off again. We reached __Las Portes des Beaujolais Anse__. The site was lovely, up on a bank near the river. Part of the campground could be used for flood control for the R. Saone. There is a minature railway into town. The boys swam in the pool. Eve did a load of wash £€4. There are again lots of Dutch people who are very pleasant to camp with. lots of wooden cabins in the bushes.

	__Friday__
	At elevenses we arrived at the Gras house in the Mont d'Or north of Lyons. John + Charles will do an exchange with Rafael + Victor. So the 4 boys spent the day together with Fran + Danny, while Simon + Eve went sightseeing in Lyons. We saw the Musée des Beaux Arts, which had been closed for 8 years

for refurbishment. The art is beautifully laid out. We saw Egyptian things, including an axe, Mediaeval ivories + lovely paintings. We saw St. Nizier's church + walked across a red footbridge to the Cathedral. There was an amusing picture on a restaurant window of St. George sharing a bottle of wine, seated at a table, with a dragon, + the dragon was gobbing the chicken between them.

	Danny + Fran gave us a super bbq in the evening. Eve had a bath. The boys went cycling.
	__Saturday__
	We had a relaxed breakfast. We failed to get the email to work to reach William. We left + had lunch in a park, v. modern, + then went to the Lumieres Museum + learned about early cinematography. Old silent French movies. 1895. The gardener being doused with water by the garden hose.
	After tea in the carpark we left + had much trouble finding Heyrieux, Mas de la Foret
	We had no wine at supper and at 9.30pm we left off for Lyons airport to meet Peter.

captions: Le Puy en Velay 650 m. St. Michel Rocher d'Aiguilhe
pages.text: Eve had to drive because Simon only had his dark glasses + it was dark. It seemed a long way with lots of roundabouts, + we were all glad when we got back safely. We had cup-a-soup + there was lots of rain in the night + early morning.
St. Michel d'Aiguilhe (XIe  porche roman

Sunday.  We were all tired.  the rain poured.  After Breakfast of very stale bread the rain lessened + we set off.  We found a bakery, bought croissants + bread + had a second breakfast.
	We had a late lunch at Puy en Velay.  Then we climbed up the amazing hill to the chapel.  The "hill" is really the plug of an old volcano that has eroded away.  Romanesque  After a cup of tea we drove on [the?] to [change?] to campsite hes __Terrasses du Lac__  ****  [illegible] Lacde Neus[?]  at [?Langoues].  Lots of things to do, on the side of a hill, ping pong (old) pool, ping pong, tennis, riding, sailing, wind surfing.  A fantastic view of the setting sun across the lake.  The lake is rounder shape than the last one, about the same size, but not as interesting to swim/boat in.  Further to walk to it.
	Peter chopped the Salad for supper.  Eve played ping pong with John.  Simon took photos.  Charles washed his sandals, which stank.
Monday
	We had a very relaxing day, reading + playing ping pong.  There is a large, 3-sided barn over the 2 ping pong tables.  The boys

joined in games of round-robin with other children. In the late afternoon we walked to the barage, the damn, which is just over a mile away. Brown cows, fishermen. A huge squame of water. Like Sizewell power station, it is closed to visitors, maybe because of Sept 11. On the way back there were huge storm clouds, but rain didn't fall on us.

   The supermarkets were closed yesterday & we had very little food left, so we went to the lakeside restaurant for supper & had good salad & icecream.

   __Tuesday.__

   Good croissants & bread from the reception. Peter had marmite on his croissant. There were waves of torrential rain in the early morning. Rivers inside the tent. Wet sleeping bags.

   We shopped at Mende & then found the campsite __Le Jardin de Tivoli__ * * * * ½. There was a bad row as we arrived. We had lunch & read. In the afternoon David, Sally, Maia, Dylan & Jem arrived. The children swam. David cooked spaghetti. We walked up into Mende in the evening & went to the market. It was dark when we returned. Lovely old houses [sketch of house shape] with roofs this shape & an old bridge.

   (campsite bad sewage smells at [illegible]

   [illegible] at one point.

pages.sketch_desc: A pen-and-ink drawing of a stone arch bridge, viewed from the side, showing the arch, stonework crenellations on top, and a river/rocky banks below. Captioned '(13 bridge at Mende. Still used by cars.' — likely a 13th-century bridge at Mende.
captions: (13 bridge at Mende. Still used by cars.
pages.text:
(13 bridge at Mende. Still used by cars.

   __Wednesday__

   Still quite cool weather. A pity, because the children don't swim. The boys fight at intervals. Dylan, John & Sally & Peter stayed in camp. David & Simon, Charles, [?Ella] Maia & Jem went to the supermarket in Mende & Eve got a lift in & went to the cathedral. Plain early Romanesque. A black Madonna that looked like a man. Good wood carvings on choir, & wood carvings on choir loft & Aubusson hangings up in chancel which were too dark to see.

   We had lunch back at the camp & then Simon drove us up into the hills for a walk-

captions: Jem + Maia + Eve in back of camping van, - Jem put his hand on the log
pages.text:
Jem + Maia + Eve in back of camping van, - Jem put his hand on the log

[hand tracing drawing]

Jem's hand
August 2002
Mende France

3-4 miles. It was a beautiful walk past farm houses, near Vaiserelle. Chickens, rabbits, vegetable gardens. Lovely [strikethrough] unspoitt farmhouses lanes, old roads, moss. Trees, purple thistles with grasshoppers + yellow beetles.
    We got back to camp + John + Eve made salad + Simon cooked a bbq. Yop for pud. The boys played football with a 10 year old girl from Nimes who was very good at football.

__Thursday August 8__
    We had breakfast together + packed up + said goodbye to David + family. We had lunch at a supermarket + drove on to __La Couvertoirade__. A wall was built around it by the Knights of St. John who took over from the Templars in 1450. In the 1750 it had 648 people, but the upland plateau village shrunk, so by 1880, there were only 352 inhabitants. [?And] Old disc crosses in the church + graveyard. Interesting old buildings. Simon got cross at all the gift shops. We had tea.
    We drove on to __Cirque de [?Navacolles]__.
6,000 years ago a large curve in the river had almost produced an ox-bow lake, then the river broke through back on course. 300m [?] ft. There was an amazing view from the ridge. A canal was below, too. We went on down a zig-zag road, + Eve was cross at the steep drops.
    We went alongside the canal taking water to the power station at Madières. It was like a Madeira Levada.
    We got a patisserie for supper. We drove on + on to beyond __Ganges__ 5 k south, near a river. 2 campsites have been full, but we have been allowed to spend the night outside the site + use

the loos. The rough land is actually quite nice, next to a vineyard, more peaceful than the crowded site, but expensive 19€.

__Friday__
   We set off as soon as possible. A listed camp[site] [3 km from Bri[?]ay, 1★ + ★★] was full, but we found an unlisted one [?la te[?] R. [?Hérault]] which would have us for one night. Very few facilities, but paths down to the river. The river is green + has minnows, but the boys complain that it smells like the Meare. Peter + Charles went out on the yellow rubber dingy before lunch. Charles + Simon took the 2 dinghies after lunch. There is a restaurant up at the barrage, a short walk away (dam!). We went to the restaurant for supper, but there was a band there, + no room for us because we hadn't booked. We drove to ~~the~~ local village, ~~Brossac~~ __St. Bazille de Puton__, a carnival + fairground had filled up the places at the first 2 restaurants we tried, but we finally found somewhere to eat. Duck + steak, John didn't like his first course sea snails. After supper the boys went on dodgems. There was a rock group on stage. Eve drove back to the camp. Tall acacia trees in campsite, a bit like [?redwood campsite] in [Cal[?]].

pages.sketch_desc: Small hand-drawn sketch in top-left corner of right page showing what appears to be a rough map with coastline or river outlines and a label (illegible). The main drawing is a detailed pen sketch of a classical building (old house) viewed from across a river — captioned as the view from the campsite beach on R. Hérault.
captions: View of old house across the river from campsite beach. R. Hérault.
pages.text:
[Hand-drawn sketch of an old building/house, with a small map sketch in top-left corner]

View of old house across the river from campsite beach. R. Hérault.

__Saturday__
   Sunshine as we set off. I had to wear a sweatshirt most of the day yesterday. The last few days have been sunny at [?bes] breakfast + then clouds have come in. The area round __la Couvertoirade__ was like Wales, whereas the country that we travelled in today was like south of Hurst Castle in California, before we got to the coast.
   We found a campsite __Bastide__ ★★★½ a 30 min bus ride outside __Nimes__. Having set up the tent we bussed into the city + had lunch. We saw the Maison Carrée, a well-preserved [?ho[?]se] that would [hall]

have been part of the forum. Unfortunately the Amphitheatre was closed because they were removing sets from Verdi's 'Nambucco', so we couldn't go inside.
	We checked emails at a cybercafe. Apparently William is back from S. America because he wanted to start his dissertation & he nearly got mugged by

captions: le Pont du Gard — monument romain
pages.text:
a man with a gun. The boys played on computer games. There was a fat computer nerd in charge of the cafe. Simon & Eve walked around. Eve bought a skirt. We all went to the Jardin de la Fontaine, which was rich in trees. It didn't have a fountain, but a bubbling spring, carried in from the hills.
				Marble Streets. I like the city.

Pont du Gard. Roman aqueduct built 2000 years ago by Agrippa, to take water from Uzès to Nîmes. 48.77 m high.

We had supper. A famous Nimes dish is "Brandade de Nimes" stir & barter. Breton fishermen [?battered] dried cod with salt & olive oil from Nimes. The dish was cod, milk & olive oil boiled until it was a paste, served in a ramekin with cheese on top. The rest of the food was not good. Buses stopped at 8pm. It took us [strikethrough: no] nearly an hour to find a taxi to take us back to the campsite. Eve had a migraine.

__Sunday__ Simon & Charles had bad tummies.

More sun when we woke. Big shower rooms. Simon & Eve went to church at Margueritas while Peter read & John & Charles walked to the Bread Shop. For lunch we had a mega chocolate cake. 16F. Within an hour John wanted an icecream.

In the afternoon we walked up to the __Pont du Gard__, which is spectacular. The slope is 1/300. It's altogether 30 miles long. A paper boat would take 4 minutes to sail 10ms on the flow. In 24 hours it would fill 100 Blue Coat swimming pools. The top arch carries a large, enclosed canal. It could no longer be used because it got [?silted] up.

Very windy, lots of clouds. Some dark grey. John & Charles are fighting a lot.

We arrived at our old camp site at the [?Ardèche] only to find it was full, & the one next to it.... We decided to book canoes for the next day, & up the slope we found __Raymond__. The woman in charge said we could stay in her field overnight if we were using her boats the next day. We paid for the boats & to stay overnight in the second livourne, Gournier. The whole trip came to 141€, £90. Charles swam in the river, watched by Peter. John & Simon & Eve bought bread & meat & 6 bottles of drinking water in the village. We had supper at the boat hire place. A man who smoked a pipe & had dirty hands cooked & served the food. Simon had mussels. The people were friendly, but the place was grubby. They had wallabies in a pen.
__Monday__ At 8.30 we were kitted out with paddles, life jackets & stabs/barrels. The woman [?drove] us up to the Pont d'Arc & dropped us off. It was not as hot as the time we came before, 12 years ago.
  We paddled all day, slept at Gournier, & paddled on to S. Martin & arrived at 1.30 pm. We had a very [?leisurely] first day. Charles pointed out beavers, Castor, to Eve. Peter was the only one of us who didn't capsize and rapid, which was just as well, because he didn't take shoes on the trip & wouldn't do up his life jacket.

boat. Peter + John messed about in their canoe so much that it capsized. The [?tarpaulin] lid was not on straight + water got onto P's + C's sleeping bags + Simon's + Eve's shirts. So at tea time we spread them out on rocks + bushes for an hour to [?dry] dry. Peter + John had 2 barrels on their canoe. The other canoe had one.

     Just before the camp Simon + John + Charles capsized. Simon hurt his back a bit skidding down the rapids with the canoe full of water. Charles slithered down the rapids losing his oar. Peter + Eve should have gone back to help, but we didn't realise how bad it was, + we'd gone ahead to try to find a good space for the groundsheet at the campsite. Simon took ages coming.

     We spread out 2 ground sheets. (There were other people without tents, but not many.) Simon cooked [?the] lamb + sausages on the bbq, + we had bread + water + Yop, + 4 year old prunes + raisins. The charcoal was free. We only had 3 mats, so Peter + John + Charles put 2 cross-wise for their shoulders + hips. Eve had one mat + Simon slept on the life jackets. Eve's shorts that she wanted to wear in bed had got wet, but she dried them on the bbq after the cooking. We wrapped everything in bin liners the next day to keep them dry because of the leaky barrels.

Peter had thrown a fit at the reckless thought of sleeping without a tent + had insisted that we take along bin liners in case it rained. Simon took Charles) for a walk before bed time to calm him down, + someone had retrieved his paddle + left it under the camp sign, so we were lucky to get it back. The boss were clean.

     The shooting stars were beautiful. We took a while to get to sleep. Simon + Eve both thought aching hips would keep them awake all night. In the end we slept quite well. We were all hungry for breakfast, especially Charles, who seems to have been hungry the whole holiday. We had salami, apricots + old bread + water for breakfast.

__Tuesday__
     After setting off, Simon + Eve + Charles capsized at the next 2 rapids. It was cold + tiring. Peter + John helped right the boat. Simon remarked that the trip is easy until you capsize then you feel the power of the water. Apparently during floods the Ardèche flows at 6,000 cu. m. per second. That's the weight of 3,000 camping vans per second. Nothing can withstand it. We passed one place where a canoe had got bent round a rock.

We had elevenses of shortbread & water. There are big fish in the water. When the wind was against us we had to paddle hard, but when the wind was with us, we used our paddles as sails. The trip was super, tiring, Simon & I wondered how we had ever managed to do it in one day with younger children - Thomas 10, William 8 & Peter 7. (We passed the nudist camping. I said to Charles that friends from 8'ham might be there, but people never [illegible] later we passed, he looked, we were [illegible] to [illegible] [illegible])
 We had a large lunch in St. Martin [?] & crêpes. We all felt tired. Then we drove north up the Rhône beyond Valence to Tain l'Hermitage. We camped at __Luc__ campsite, between a busy road & r. Rhône. There was a lovely view of the Rhône from the path outside the campsite, but the traffic fumes were bad. Eve washed clothes. Beautiful vineyards all around on the hill slopes & from the van we could see the large sign "Paul Jaboulet Ainé". There was a swimming pool, but only Charles & Simon would swim because Peter & John wouldn't borrow the little French [?sem] [?seven] suits. We hadn't bought food, so we had very good chicken & ice cream in the swimming pool snack bar.

__Wednesday__
	Simon walked into the village & bought bread & nice fresh croissants for breakfast. We are motoring north up the Rhône. We have to pay motorway tolls, & the boys are interested in the new Euros from different countries.
	We stopped at a supermarket to have lunch & shop, Villefranche-sur-Saône (Géant.)
	We arrived at St. Jean de Vaux at about 5. The swimming pool is fed from a spring & cold, but clean & chlorine-free. One of the nicest swimming pools to swim in, open 11-1, 3-7. We all swam, except Peter. Then Simon & Eve walked around the village, looking at the 3 wine houses, & walking down a track to the old mill. This had been converted into a private house with a big weeping willow in front, so we couldn't see it. There are lots of tents & a large coach in the camp from the Czech republic.
__Thursday__ We booked a meal at the feter that is to take place in the camp in the evening. All of us went wine tasting at Chaumont, where we bought red wine, & Isaiah, where we bought white. We borrowed a trolley, un diable, from Isaiah to wheel it back to [the camp].

pages.sketch_desc: Outline map of France. Labels: 'P' near north-centre (likely Paris), 'CF' and 'L' in the centre-south area (possibly Clermont-Ferrand and Lyon or similar). No roads or arrows drawn, just the country outline with dot-markers.
pages.text:
2002

[hand-drawn outline map of France with labels: "P" (north-centre), "CF" and "L" (centre-south area)]

van (7 boxes).
	We had lunch & swam. Simon & Eve went for another walk & found Gite André, a lovely place to stay. They wanted to exchange children & houses. Both teachers. She's Catholic, he's Protestant, & moving away to teach in a protestant school.
	At 8 we went to the fetes where we ate on trestle tables next to friendly people. There was Karaoke Peter remarked that the French have a large body of

songs that are known by all ages, but the English wouldn't be able to do this. We had delicious salads, ham & Sauce & potatoes, bread, white cheese & left before the Tarte Tartin. Very noisy & great [?fun/run] fun.
	__Friday__ Again, lovely sunshine. We drove north for hours and hours, having lunch & tea on [?fires/fires]. We have arrived at __Lumbres__. We had herring for supper. The boys have been playing Irish tin whistles to drown out the Belgians' 60's pop music from next door. He has been across & sung us the Walloon national anthem, but wouldn't try it on the tin whistle. Simon has come back from the loos with wet feet & warned us not to flush the loos here.
	__Saturday August 17th__
		The campsite is not very clean. Litter left around & bins overflowing. But the site with tall trees could be good if it were well run. We set off about 9.15 for the 11.00 Calais-Dover ferry.
		In fact we caught the 10.15 ferry & crossed in warm sunshine. At 1.15 we reached Jane [?Portal/Portral] in her [?Red/Reed] flat. We had lunch with her & Rosie. Simon mended Rosie's new pogo stick & gave his goddaughter a bathroom radio for her [?14th/?4th] [illegible]
